DISCOVERY 302, THERE BEING A NON-STIP TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM, PLAINTIFF GILLIAN ASHDOWN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 4, 2018 AT 9:30 A.M., LAW & MOTION. LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF GILLIAN ASHDOWN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED. PER THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN STEIN, THE ONLY REMAINING ISSUE IN DISPUTE ON THIS MOTION IS MS. ASHDOWN'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. THAT REQUEST IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT ALEXANDER PETERSON WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE DISCOVERY AT ISSUE IN THIS MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 (302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Set For Trial On Jun-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph S. May, Esq, of The Law Office Of Joseph S. May, Tel. 415.781.3333, appearing for the plaintiff and moving party Gillian Ashdown;
Alexander Peterson, Defendant, appearing in propria persona.

Both sides stipulate that the matter can be heard by Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish.

Matter called. Plaintiff Gillian Ashdown's Notice of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions

The parties contested the tentative ruling as to the issue of sanctions and the parties appeared at the hearing.

A R G U M E N T held. Ruling - At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as modified with regard to the sanctions only: Sanctions are awarded against defendant in the amount of $600. The discovery at issue in this motion was not burdensome - simply contact information for two witnesses identified in defendant's discovery responses. Plaintiff offered defendant additional time to provide this information if defendant would agree to a corresponding extension of time for a motion to compel. Defendant refused, and did not provide his further response to the interrogatories at issue until two weeks after plaintiff's motion was filed. Order granting the motion is signed as modified in open court. (Plaintiff's Motion For Protective Order is denied. Plaintiff failed to meet his burden to show good cause for the order sought. (Fairmont Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (2000) Cal. 4th 245, 255.) Further, the preponderance of the evidence indicates plaintiff raised questions of mail service to negotiate other matters. Sanctions are awarded against plaintiff in the amount of $450. Order signed as modified in open court. (See order filed this date for full terms.)

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Gillian Ashdown's motion to compel responses to discovery and for monetary sanctions is denied. Per the report of Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, the only remaining issue in dispute on this motion is Ms. Ashdown's request for monetary sanctions. That request is denied because defendant Alexander Peterson was not properly served with the discovery at issue in this motion. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
